title: Optical Payload TVAC
summary: Thermal-vacuum functional and optical performance verification for an imaging payload using controlled stimulus and contamination monitoring.

steps.title: Confirm payload configuration
steps.description: Verify hardware and software configuration, cleanliness status, red-tag and green-tag items, optical covers, purge history, and the approved test procedure revision.

steps.title: Establish contamination controls
steps.description: Inspect accessible optical surfaces under the approved method and install authorized witness samples, quartz-crystal microbalance monitoring, molecular adsorbers, or covers.

steps.title: Verify mechanical installation
steps.description: Confirm mounting interfaces, fastener witness marks, gravity-offload provisions if applicable, thermal straps, heater interfaces, harness strain relief, grounding, and purge connections.

images.alt: Technician installing an imaging spectrometer into a thermal vacuum chamber.
images.caption: Reference installation view for checking payload support, harness routing, chamber clearances, and handling access before closeout.

steps.title: Validate instrumentation
steps.description: Verify temperature-sensor placement and polarity, heater channels, chamber instrumentation, EGSE calibration, optical stimulus telemetry, data paths, and common time synchronization.

steps.title: Align optical stimulus
steps.description: Align and document the collimator, target, integrating source, or star simulator through the chamber optical path. Record reference geometry, focus setting, and boresight datum.

images.alt: Optical coronagraph test chamber and optical bench hardware at NASA JPL.
images.caption: Reference optical test arrangement illustrating a controlled chamber path used with a simulated star source and precision optical hardware.

steps.title: Capture ambient baseline
steps.description: Acquire pre-test functional, dark, noise, radiometric, focus, boresight, distortion, image-quality, mechanism, and housekeeping telemetry data under the approved ambient condition.

steps.title: Close and pump chamber
steps.description: Complete FOD accountability and chamber closeout, then pump down while monitoring pressure, payload temperatures, purge transition, outgassing, and contamination instrumentation.

steps.title: Transition to cold plateau
steps.description: Command the approved cold transition while monitoring ramp rate, gradients, survival limits, heater control, and chamber-to-payload thermal response.

steps.title: Test at cold stabilization
steps.description: After approved stability criteria are satisfied, execute cold functional checks and acquire the specified dark, radiometric, focus, boresight, distortion, and image-quality datasets.

steps.title: Exercise payload mechanisms
steps.description: Operate shutters, filter wheels, focus mechanisms, calibration sources, doors, heaters, and safe-mode functions only at the prescribed thermal states; record current and timing signatures.

steps.title: Test at hot stabilization
steps.description: At the approved hot stabilized condition, repeat functional and optical performance measurements and compare key telemetry against predicted thermal behavior.

steps.title: Execute thermal cycles
steps.description: Complete the prescribed hot and cold cycles, intermediate operational profiles, non-operating dwells, and required performance samples without exceeding approved ramps or gradients.

steps.title: Assess thermo-optical stability
steps.description: Evaluate focus, boresight, distortion, radiometric response, detector behavior, image quality, and alignment repeatability across temperature using the controlled optical reference.

steps.title: Repeat ambient-vacuum baseline
steps.description: Return to the approved ambient-vacuum condition and repeat the baseline dataset to identify hysteresis, permanent alignment shift, contamination effects, or performance drift.

steps.title: Vent and perform closeout
steps.description: Vent with the approved clean, dry gas sequence. Inspect optical and mechanical interfaces, review witness and contamination data, reconcile anomalies, archive evidence, and restore configuration.
